findings.title: Deployment can embed global operators with authority to move or burn every holder's tokens

best_agent_match.title: Deployment-time default operators can drain or burn every holder's balance

findings.title: ERC20-looking transfers still execute ERC777 recipient hooks, enabling callback reentrancy in integrators

best_agent_match.title: ERC20-looking transfers still execute recipient callbacks, enabling reentrancy against integrators

findings.title: Sender-side ERC777 hooks run before balances are debited, exposing pull and burn flows to pre-state reentrancy

best_agent_match.title: Sender-side ERC777 hook fires before debiting balances, enabling pre-state reentrancy

rejected_candidates.title: ERC20 entrypoints bypass recipient acknowledgment and can permanently lock tokens in contracts
rejected_candidates.reason: This is intentional ERC20-compatibility behavior inherited from OpenZeppelin ERC777: `transfer`/`transferFrom` are allowed to send into non-ERC777 contracts just like normal ERC20s. Any lockup depends on the recipient contract lacking an exit path, which is an integration/user mistake rather than a token-specific flaw.

rejected_candidates.title: Allowance changes are vulnerable to the standard ERC20 approval race
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the well-known ERC20 allowance race from overwrite-style `approve`, with standard zero-reset mitigation. It is not a custom bug in this implementation and is generally treated as non-reportable absent unusual token-specific behavior.

rejected_candidates.title: Hard-coded ERC1820 registry dependency can brick the token on networks without the registry
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic ERC777 deployment prerequisite and portability concern, not a vulnerability in the deployed token instance absent evidence it is intended for a network without the canonical ERC1820 registry.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited Token Minting Through Inheritance
rejected_candidates.reason: `_mint` is `internal`, but inheriting from this source code and deploying a child contract would create a different token contract, not mint more balances into the already-deployed `n00d` at `0x2321537fd8ef4644bacdceec54e5f35bf44311fa`.

rejected_candidates.title: ERC777 Hooks Can Cause Permanent Transfer Locks
rejected_candidates.reason: A sender or recipient hook can only block transfers involving the account that voluntarily registered that hook. That is opt-in/self-griefing behavior defined by ERC777, not a realistic protocol-level vulnerability in this token.
